history.content:
<thought>
I am at (row1, col1) facing east. Directly ahead is lava, so I cannot move forward. Turning left would face north into a wall, so the only safe turn is right to face south. This allows me to move down and then navigate around the lava to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at position (1,1) facing down. The goal is at (3,3). Moving forward one step will take me to (2,1), which is a floor cell and safe. I will then need to turn left to face right and continue towards the goal. So the immediate action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>I am at (2,1) facing down. The goal is at (3,3) but there is lava at (3,2). To avoid it, I can go right first, then down. So I need to turn left to face right.</thought>
<action>TURN_LEFT</action>

<thought>
I am at position (row2, col1) facing right. The goal is at (row3, col3). I can move forward to the empty cell directly ahead, then continue right, then turn right and move down to reach the goal. So the next action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at (row2, col2) facing right. The goal is directly to the southeast at (row3, col3). Moving forward will take me to (row2, col3), which is empty and one step closer horizontally. Then I can turn right to face down and move to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 2, column 3, facing right. The goal is at (3,3) directly below me. I cannot move forward because there is a wall ahead. I need to turn to face down. Turning right clockwise from right to down will achieve that. Then I can move forward to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 2, column 3 facing down. The goal G is directly below me at (3,3). The cell in front is the goal, not a wall or lava. So moving forward will reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
